## ars.R
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) for univariate log-concave densities.
##
## Implements the tangent-based upper-hull / chord-based lower-hull algorithm of
##   Gilks, W. R., & Wild, P. (1992). Adaptive rejection sampling for Gibbs
##   sampling. JRSS-C (Applied Statistics), 41(2), 337-348.
##
## Design notes
##   * Functional style throughout: `ars()` is the public entry point and all
##     discrete sub-tasks live in small, prefixed (`.`) auxiliary functions.
##   * The user supplies a *vectorised* (possibly unnormalised) DENSITY.  The
##     log-density h = log f and its derivative h' are handled internally; h' is
##     obtained by central finite differences so the caller need not supply it.
##   * Log-concavity is verified continuously during sampling: (a) the tangent
##     slopes must be non-increasing in x, and (b) the piecewise-linear upper
##     hull must dominate h wherever h is evaluated.  Either violation aborts
##     with an informative error.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

## ---- log-density and its numerical derivative ------------------------------

## Wrap a (possibly unnormalised) density as a safe log-density.
.make_logdensity <- function(density_function) {
  function(x) {
    d <- density_function(x)
    out <- ifelse(is.finite(d) & d > 0, log(d), -Inf)
    as.numeric(out)
  }
}

## Central-difference derivative of the log-density, with one-sided fallbacks
## when a neighbour falls outside the support (h = -Inf there).
.make_derivative <- function(h) {
  function(x) {
    eps <- 1e-5 * max(1, abs(x))
    fp <- h(x + eps)
    fm <- h(x - eps)
    hp <- (fp - fm) / (2 * eps)
    if (!is.finite(hp)) {
      f0 <- h(x)
      hp <- (fp - f0) / eps
      if (!is.finite(hp)) hp <- (f0 - fm) / eps
    }
    hp
  }
}

## ---- input validation ------------------------------------------------------

.validate_inputs <- function(density_function, domain, n) {
  if (!is.function(density_function))
    stop("`density_function` must be a function returning the (unnormalised) density.")
  if (!is.numeric(domain) || length(domain) != 2L)
    stop("`domain` must be a numeric vector of length 2, e.g. c(lower, upper).")
  if (any(is.na(domain)))
    stop("`domain` must not contain NA.")
  if (domain[1] >= domain[2])
    stop("`domain` lower bound must be strictly less than the upper bound.")
  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1L || is.na(n))
    stop("`n` must be a single number.")
  if (n <= 0)
    stop("`n` must be a positive integer (cannot sample a non-positive count).")
  if (n != round(n))
    stop("`n` must be a whole number.")

  ## Probe the density: it must be vectorised and non-negative.
  fin <- domain[is.finite(domain)]
  probe <- if (length(fin) == 2) c(mean(fin), fin[1] + diff(fin) / 4)
           else if (length(fin) == 1) c(fin, fin + 1, fin - 1)
           else c(-1, 0, 1)
  vals <- tryCatch(density_function(probe),
                   error = function(e)
                     stop("`density_function` failed to evaluate: ", conditionMessage(e)))
  if (length(vals) != length(probe))
    stop("`density_function` must be vectorised (return one value per input element).")
  if (any(is.finite(vals) & vals < 0))
    stop("`density_function` returned negative values; a density must be non-negative.")
  invisible(TRUE)
}

## ---- initialisation of the starting abscissae ------------------------------

## Choose an initial set of points.  For an unbounded side we must find a point
## whose tangent slope points "inward" (positive slope on the left, negative on
## the right) so that the upper hull encloses finite area.
.initialize_abscissae <- function(h, hprime, domain, max_step = 1e8) {
  lb <- domain[1]; ub <- domain[2]
  finite_h <- function(x) is.finite(x) && is.finite(h(x))

  ## anchor: any interior point with positive density
  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub))      cand <- (lb + ub) / 2
  else if (is.finite(lb))                  cand <- lb + c(1, 0.5, 2, 5, 10)
  else if (is.finite(ub))                  cand <- ub - c(1, 0.5, 2, 5, 10)
  else                                     cand <- c(0, 1, -1, 5, -5, 20, -20)
  anchor <- NA_real_
  for (c0 in cand) if (finite_h(c0)) { anchor <- c0; break }
  if (is.na(anchor))
    stop("Could not locate a point with positive density inside `domain`; ",
         "check `density_function` and `domain`.")

  ## left abscissa
  if (is.finite(lb)) {
    delta <- min(1, if (is.finite(ub)) (ub - lb) / 4 else 1)
    xl <- lb + delta; tries <- 0
    while ((!finite_h(xl) || xl >= anchor) && tries < 200) {
      delta <- delta / 2; xl <- lb + delta; tries <- tries + 1
    }
  } else {
    step <- 1; xl <- anchor - step
    while ((!finite_h(xl) || hprime(xl) <= 0) && step < max_step) {
      step <- step * 2; xl <- anchor - step
    }
    if (!(finite_h(xl) && hprime(xl) > 0))
      stop("Could not initialise a left abscissa with positive slope; the density ",
           "may be non-log-concave or not integrable over an unbounded left tail.")
  }

  ## right abscissa
  if (is.finite(ub)) {
    delta <- min(1, if (is.finite(lb)) (ub - lb) / 4 else 1)
    xr <- ub - delta; tries <- 0
    while ((!finite_h(xr) || xr <= anchor) && tries < 200) {
      delta <- delta / 2; xr <- ub - delta; tries <- tries + 1
    }
  } else {
    step <- 1; xr <- anchor + step
    while ((!finite_h(xr) || hprime(xr) >= 0) && step < max_step) {
      step <- step * 2; xr <- anchor + step
    }
    if (!(finite_h(xr) && hprime(xr) < 0))
      stop("Could not initialise a right abscissa with negative slope; the density ",
           "may be non-log-concave or not integrable over an unbounded right tail.")
  }

  pts <- sort(unique(c(xl, anchor, xr)))
  pts <- pts[vapply(pts, finite_h, logical(1))]
  if (length(pts) < 2)
    stop("Failed to initialise adaptive-rejection abscissae for this density/domain.")
  pts
}

## ---- hull construction -----------------------------------------------------

## Build the upper (tangent) and lower (chord) hulls from the current abscissae
## and cache the per-segment integrals used for sampling.  This is also where
## log-concavity is enforced via the monotone-slope condition.
.build_hull <- function(x, hx, hpx, domain, tol = 1e-6) {
  o <- order(x); x <- x[o]; hx <- hx[o]; hpx <- hpx[o]
  k <- length(x)
  lb <- domain[1]; ub <- domain[2]

  ## (a) log-concavity: tangent slopes must be non-increasing in x.
  if (k >= 2) {
    slope_tol <- tol * (1 + max(abs(hpx)))
    if (any(diff(hpx) > slope_tol))
      stop("Density is not log-concave: the log-density derivative increases ",
           "with x (tangent slopes are not monotone non-increasing).")
  }
  ## integrability on unbounded sides
  if (is.infinite(lb) && hpx[1] <= 0)
    stop("Density is not integrable / not log-concave: log-density does not ",
         "decrease toward -Inf.")
  if (is.infinite(ub) && hpx[k] >= 0)
    stop("Density is not integrable / not log-concave: log-density does not ",
         "decrease toward +Inf.")

  ## intersection points z_j of adjacent tangents (length k-1)
  z <- numeric(max(k - 1L, 0L))
  if (k >= 2) for (j in seq_len(k - 1L)) {
    denom <- hpx[j] - hpx[j + 1]
    z[j] <- if (abs(denom) < 1e-12) (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2
            else (hx[j + 1] - hx[j] - x[j + 1] * hpx[j + 1] + x[j] * hpx[j]) / denom
  }
  seg_lo <- c(lb, z)
  seg_hi <- c(z, ub)

  ## per-segment upper-hull values at the endpoints (scaled by M for stability)
  u_lo <- hx + (seg_lo - x) * hpx
  u_hi <- hx + (seg_hi - x) * hpx
  M <- max(c(u_lo[is.finite(u_lo)], u_hi[is.finite(u_hi)]))

  eps_m <- 1e-10
  area <- numeric(k)
  for (j in seq_len(k)) {
    m <- hpx[j]
    if (abs(m) < eps_m) {
      uc <- hx[j] + ((seg_lo[j] + seg_hi[j]) / 2 - x[j]) * m
      area[j] <- exp(uc - M) * (seg_hi[j] - seg_lo[j])
    } else {
      area[j] <- (exp(u_hi[j] - M) - exp(u_lo[j] - M)) / m
    }
  }
  area[!is.finite(area) | area < 0] <- 0
  if (sum(area) <= 0)
    stop("Upper hull has non-positive total area; sampling cannot proceed.")

  list(x = x, hx = hx, hpx = hpx, z = z,
       seg_lo = seg_lo, seg_hi = seg_hi, area = area, M = M,
       lb = lb, ub = ub, k = k)
}

## ---- hull evaluation -------------------------------------------------------

## Upper hull u_k(x): the enclosing tangent for the segment containing x.
.upper_eval <- function(hull, xstar) {
  j <- findInterval(xstar, hull$z) + 1L
  j <- min(max(j, 1L), hull$k)
  hull$hx[j] + (xstar - hull$x[j]) * hull$hpx[j]
}

## Lower hull l_k(x): chord between the bracketing abscissae; -Inf outside them.
.lower_eval <- function(hull, xstar) {
  x <- hull$x; k <- hull$k
  if (xstar < x[1] || xstar > x[k]) return(-Inf)
  j <- findInterval(xstar, x)
  if (j < 1L) j <- 1L
  if (j >= k) j <- k - 1L
  xl <- x[j]; xr <- x[j + 1]
  ((xr - xstar) * hull$hx[j] + (xstar - xl) * hull$hx[j + 1]) / (xr - xl)
}

## ---- sampling from the upper hull -----------------------------------------

## Draw one candidate x* from the density proportional to exp(u_k(x)).
## Each segment is a truncated exponential; we pick a segment by its mass and
## then invert its CDF in a numerically stable, overflow-free manner.
.sample_upper <- function(hull) {
  j <- sample.int(hull$k, 1L, prob = hull$area / sum(hull$area))
  a <- hull$seg_lo[j]; b <- hull$seg_hi[j]; m <- hull$hpx[j]
  U <- runif(1)
  eps_m <- 1e-10
  if (abs(m) < eps_m) {
    a + U * (b - a)
  } else if (m > 0) {
    q <- if (is.infinite(a)) 0 else exp(m * (a - b))   # reference = finite b
    b + log(q + U * (1 - q)) / m
  } else {
    q <- if (is.infinite(b)) 0 else exp(m * (b - a))   # reference = finite a
    a + log(1 + U * (q - 1)) / m
  }
}

## ---- public entry point ----------------------------------------------------

#' Adaptive rejection sampler.
#'
#' @param density_function vectorised, (possibly unnormalised) log-concave density.
#' @param domain length-2 numeric c(lower, upper); infinite bounds allowed.
#' @param n number of samples to draw (positive integer).
#' @return numeric vector of length `n` drawn from the target density.
ars <- function(density_function, domain, n = 1000L) {
  .validate_inputs(density_function, domain, n)
  n <- as.integer(n)

  h      <- .make_logdensity(density_function)
  hprime <- .make_derivative(h)

  x0  <- .initialize_abscissae(h, hprime, domain)
  hx  <- vapply(x0, h, numeric(1))
  hpx <- vapply(x0, hprime, numeric(1))
  hull <- .build_hull(x0, hx, hpx, domain)

  samples <- numeric(n)
  count <- 0L
  iter <- 0L
  max_iter <- as.numeric(n) * 1000 + 1e5

  while (count < n) {
    iter <- iter + 1L
    if (iter > max_iter)
      stop("ARS did not converge within the iteration budget; the density may be ",
           "pathological or extremely peaked.")

    xstar <- .sample_upper(hull)
    if (!is.finite(xstar) || xstar <= hull$lb || xstar >= hull$ub) next

    u_val <- .upper_eval(hull, xstar)
    l_val <- .lower_eval(hull, xstar)
    logw  <- log(runif(1))

    if (logw <= l_val - u_val) {
      ## squeezing test: accept without evaluating h
      count <- count + 1L; samples[count] <- xstar
      next
    }

    ## squeezing failed -> evaluate the true log-density
    hxs <- h(xstar)
    tol <- 1e-6 * (1 + abs(u_val))
    if (hxs > u_val + tol)
      stop("Density is not log-concave: log-density exceeds its tangent upper ",
           "hull at x = ", format(xstar), ".")

    if (logw <= hxs - u_val) {
      count <- count + 1L; samples[count] <- xstar
    }

    ## adapt: add the new point (rebuild re-checks log-concavity)
    if (min(abs(hull$x - xstar)) > 1e-8) {
      hps  <- hprime(xstar)
      hull <- .build_hull(c(hull$x, xstar), c(hull$hx, hxs),
                          c(hull$hpx, hps), domain)
    }
  }
  samples
}

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Formal testing
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Compare ARS output for one distribution against known truth (mean, sd, CDF).
.test_distribution <- function(name, density_function, domain, n,
                               true_mean, true_sd, cdf, save_file = NULL) {
  cat(sprintf("[%s] drawing %d samples via ars()...\n", name, n))
  samples <- ars(density_function, domain, n)
  m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)

  ks <- suppressWarnings(stats::ks.test(samples, cdf))
  mean_ok <- abs(m - true_mean) < 5 * true_sd / sqrt(n) + 1e-3
  sd_ok   <- abs(s - true_sd)   < 0.10 * true_sd + 1e-3
  ks_ok   <- ks$p.value > 1e-3
  pass <- mean_ok && sd_ok && ks_ok

  if (!is.null(save_file)) {
    writeLines(format(samples, digits = 10), save_file)
    cat(sprintf("        wrote %d samples to %s\n", length(samples), save_file))
  }
  cat(sprintf("%s: %s  (mean=%.4f [true %.4f], sd=%.4f [true %.4f], KS p=%.4f)\n",
              name, if (pass) "PASS" else "FAIL", m, true_mean, s, true_sd, ks$p.value))
  pass
}

## Check that the hull brackets h and integrates sensibly (module-level test).
.test_hull <- function() {
  name <- "HULL_BRACKETING"
  h  <- .make_logdensity(function(x) dnorm(x))
  hp <- .make_derivative(h)
  x0  <- c(-1.5, 0, 1.5)
  hull <- .build_hull(x0, vapply(x0, h, numeric(1)),
                      vapply(x0, hp, numeric(1)), c(-Inf, Inf))
  grid <- seq(-4, 4, by = 0.25)
  up <- vapply(grid, function(g) .upper_eval(hull, g), numeric(1))
  lo <- vapply(grid, function(g) .lower_eval(hull, g), numeric(1))
  hv <- vapply(grid, h, numeric(1))
  ok_upper <- all(up >= hv - 1e-8)
  inner <- grid >= min(x0) & grid <= max(x0)
  ok_lower <- all(lo[inner] <= hv[inner] + 1e-8)
  pass <- ok_upper && ok_lower
  cat(sprintf("%s: %s  (upper>=h: %s, lower<=h on [x1,xk]: %s)\n",
              name, if (pass) "PASS" else "FAIL", ok_upper, ok_lower))
  pass
}

## Check the numerical derivative against a known analytic gradient.
.test_derivative <- function() {
  name <- "NUMERIC_DERIVATIVE"
  h  <- .make_logdensity(function(x) dnorm(x))   # h'(x) = -x
  hp <- .make_derivative(h)
  xs <- c(-2, -0.5, 0, 0.75, 2)
  err <- max(abs(vapply(xs, hp, numeric(1)) - (-xs)))
  pass <- err < 1e-4
  cat(sprintf("%s: %s  (max abs error vs analytic = %.2e)\n",
              name, if (pass) "PASS" else "FAIL", err))
  pass
}

## Check that invalid inputs are rejected.
.test_validation <- function() {
  name <- "INPUT_VALIDATION"
  threw <- function(expr) inherits(try(expr, silent = TRUE), "try-error")
  checks <- c(
    neg_n     = threw(ars(function(x) dnorm(x), c(-Inf, Inf), n = -5)),
    zero_n    = threw(ars(function(x) dnorm(x), c(-Inf, Inf), n = 0)),
    bad_dom   = threw(ars(function(x) dnorm(x), c(1, 1), n = 10)),
    dom_len   = threw(ars(function(x) dnorm(x), c(0), n = 10)),
    not_fun   = threw(ars("not a function", c(-Inf, Inf), n = 10)),
    neg_dens  = threw(ars(function(x) -1 + 0 * x, c(0, 1), n = 10))
  )
  pass <- all(checks)
  cat(sprintf("%s: %s  (%d/%d invalid inputs correctly rejected)\n",
              name, if (pass) "PASS" else "FAIL", sum(checks), length(checks)))
  pass
}

## Check that a non-log-concave density is detected and rejected.
.test_non_log_concave <- function() {
  name <- "NON_LOG_CONCAVE_DETECTION"
  ## Unnormalised Cauchy: f(x) = 1/(1+x^2) is NOT log-concave in the tails.
  err <- try(ars(function(x) 1 / (1 + x^2), c(-Inf, Inf), n = 2000), silent = TRUE)
  pass <- inherits(err, "try-error") &&
          grepl("log-concave", conditionMessage(attr(err, "condition")))
  cat(sprintf("%s: %s  (%s)\n", name, if (pass) "PASS" else "FAIL",
              if (pass) "error correctly raised" else "NOT detected"))
  pass
}

#' Run the full formal test suite, printing PASS/FAIL for each test.
#' @return invisibly, TRUE if every test passed.
test <- function() {
  set.seed(1992)
  cat("=====================================================================\n")
  cat(" Adaptive Rejection Sampling -- formal test suite (Gilks & Wild 1992)\n")
  cat("=====================================================================\n\n")

  results <- logical(0)

  cat("-- module tests -----------------------------------------------------\n")
  results["derivative"] <- .test_derivative()
  results["hull"]       <- .test_hull()
  results["validation"] <- .test_validation()
  results["concavity"]  <- .test_non_log_concave()

  cat("\n-- distribution tests (compared to known truth) ---------------------\n")
  results["normal"] <- .test_distribution(
    "NORMAL_STANDARD", function(x) dnorm(x), c(-Inf, Inf), n = 5000,
    true_mean = 0, true_sd = 1, cdf = function(q) pnorm(q),
    save_file = "/app/normal_samples.txt")

  results["normal2"] <- .test_distribution(
    "NORMAL_MU2_SD1.5", function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 2, sd = 1.5), c(-Inf, Inf),
    n = 5000, true_mean = 2, true_sd = 1.5,
    cdf = function(q) pnorm(q, mean = 2, sd = 1.5))

  results["exponential"] <- .test_distribution(
    "EXPONENTIAL_RATE1", function(x) dexp(x, rate = 1), c(0, Inf), n = 5000,
    true_mean = 1, true_sd = 1, cdf = function(q) pexp(q, rate = 1),
    save_file = "/app/exponential_samples.txt")

  results["gamma"] <- .test_distribution(
    "GAMMA_SHAPE3", function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 2), c(0, Inf),
    n = 5000, true_mean = 3 / 2, true_sd = sqrt(3) / 2,
    cdf = function(q) pgamma(q, shape = 3, rate = 2))

  results["truncnorm"] <- .test_distribution(
    "TRUNC_NORMAL_[-1,1]",
    function(x) dnorm(x), c(-1, 1), n = 5000,
    true_mean = 0,
    true_sd = sqrt(1 - (2 * dnorm(1)) / (pnorm(1) - pnorm(-1))),
    cdf = function(q) (pnorm(q) - pnorm(-1)) / (pnorm(1) - pnorm(-1)))

  cat("\n=====================================================================\n")
  cat(sprintf(" OVERALL: %d/%d tests passed -> %s\n",
              sum(results), length(results),
              if (all(results)) "ALL PASS" else "SOME FAILED"))
  cat("=====================================================================\n")
  invisible(all(results))
}

## Auto-run the suite only when executed as `Rscript ars.R`.
local({
  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = FALSE)
  file_arg <- grep("^--file=", args, value = TRUE)
  if (length(file_arg) && grepl("ars\\.R$", file_arg)) test()
})

edits.new:
if (k >= 2) for (j in seq_len(k - 1L)) {
    denom <- hpx[j] - hpx[j + 1]
    zj <- if (abs(denom) < 1e-8) (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2
          else (hx[j + 1] - hx[j] - x[j + 1] * hpx[j + 1] + x[j] * hpx[j]) / denom
    ## For a concave h the tangents meet inside [x_j, x_{j+1}]; clamp to guard
    ## against finite-difference noise on (near-)log-linear densities.
    if (!is.finite(zj)) zj <- (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2
    z[j] <- min(max(zj, x[j]), x[j + 1])
  }

## ars.R:L165-L178
    stop("Density is not integrable / not log-concave: log-density does not ",
         "decrease toward +Inf.")

  ## intersection points z_j of adjacent tangents (length k-1)
  z <- numeric(max(k - 1L, 0L))
  if (k >= 2) for (j in seq_len(k - 1L)) {
    denom <- hpx[j] - hpx[j + 1]
    z[j] <- if (abs(denom) < 1e-12) (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2
            else (hx[j + 1] - hx[j] - x[j + 1] * hpx[j + 1] + x[j] * hpx[j]) / denom
  }
  seg_lo <- c(lb, z)
  seg_hi <- c(z, ub)

  ## per-segment upper-hull values at the endpoints (scaled by M for stability)

  ## intersection points z_j of adjacent tangents (length k-1)

    z[j] <- if (abs(denom) < 1e-12) (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2

edits.new:
if (k >= 2) for (j in seq_len(k - 1L)) {
    denom <- hpx[j] - hpx[j + 1]
    zj <- if (abs(denom) < 1e-8) (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2
          else (hx[j + 1] - hx[j] - x[j + 1] * hpx[j + 1] + x[j] * hpx[j]) / denom
    ## For concave h the tangents meet inside [x_j, x_{j+1}]; clamp to guard
    ## against finite-difference noise on (near-)log-linear densities.
    if (!is.finite(zj)) zj <- (x[j] + x[j + 1]) / 2
    z[j] <- min(max(zj, x[j]), x[j + 1])
  }

content.text:
Done. Implementation complete and verified.

## Summary

`/app/ars.R` implements adaptive rejection sampling per Gilks & Wild (1992):

**Algorithm** — tangent-based upper hull + chord-based lower hull on the log-density `h = log f`:
- `.make_logdensity` / `.make_derivative` — wrap the user's vectorized density; `h'` via central finite differences (caller supplies only the density).
- `.validate_inputs` — rejects non-functions, bad domains (`lower ≥ upper`, wrong length, NA), non-positive/non-integer `n`, non-vectorized or negative densities.
- `.initialize_abscissae` — finds starting points, expanding outward on unbounded sides until slopes point inward (integrability).
- `.build_hull` — computes tangent intersections `z_j` (clamped to `[x_j,x_{j+1}]` for numerical safety), per-segment integrals with log-scaling for overflow safety; **enforces log-concavity** via the monotone-slope condition.
- `.upper_eval` / `.lower_eval` / `.sample_upper` — hull evaluation and stable inverse-CDF sampling of the piecewise-exponential envelope.
- `ars(density_function, domain, n = 1000L)` — squeeze test → rejection test → adaptive update; a second log-concavity guard fires if `h` ever exceeds its upper hull.

**Testing** (`test()`, seed-fixed, prints `TEST: PASS/FAIL` with mean/sd):
- Module tests: numeric derivative vs analytic, hull bracketing (`upper ≥ h ≥ lower`), input validation (6 bad inputs rejected), non-log-concave detection (unnormalized Cauchy → error).
- Distribution tests vs known truth (mean, sd, KS p-value): standard normal, N(2,1.5), Exp(1), Gamma(3,2), truncated normal.

**Verification** — `Rscript ars.R` → **9/9 tests PASS**; e.g. `NORMAL_STANDARD: PASS (mean=-0.0020, sd=1.0092, KS p=0.7642)`, `EXPONENTIAL_RATE1: PASS (mean=0.9999, sd=0.9910, KS p=0.7434)`. Sample files `/app/normal_samples.txt` and `/app/exponential_samples.txt` (5000 lines each) written; exponential samples all `> 0`.

Done. Implementation complete and verified.

## Summary

`/app/ars.R` implements adaptive rejection sampling per Gilks & Wild (1992):

**Algorithm** — tangent-based upper hull + chord-based lower hull on the log-density `h = log f`:
- `.make_logdensity` / `.make_derivative` — wrap the user's vectorized density; `h'` via central finite differences (caller supplies only the density).
- `.validate_inputs` — rejects non-functions, bad domains (`lower ≥ upper`, wrong length, NA), non-positive/non-integer `n`, non-vectorized or negative densities.
- `.initialize_abscissae` — finds starting points, expanding outward on unbounded sides until slopes point inward (integrability).
- `.build_hull` — computes tangent intersections `z_j` (clamped to `[x_j,x_{j+1}]` for numerical safety), per-segment integrals with log-scaling for overflow safety; **enforces log-concavity** via the monotone-slope condition.
- `.upper_eval` / `.lower_eval` / `.sample_upper` — hull evaluation and stable inverse-CDF sampling of the piecewise-exponential envelope.
- `ars(density_function, domain, n = 1000L)` — squeeze test → rejection test → adaptive update; a second log-concavity guard fires if `h` ever exceeds its upper hull.

**Testing** (`test()`, seed-fixed, prints `TEST: PASS/FAIL` with mean/sd):
- Module tests: numeric derivative vs analytic, hull bracketing (`upper ≥ h ≥ lower`), input validation (6 bad inputs rejected), non-log-concave detection (unnormalized Cauchy → error).
- Distribution tests vs known truth (mean, sd, KS p-value): standard normal, N(2,1.5), Exp(1), Gamma(3,2), truncated normal.

**Verification** — `Rscript ars.R` → **9/9 tests PASS**; e.g. `NORMAL_STANDARD: PASS (mean=-0.0020, sd=1.0092, KS p=0.7642)`, `EXPONENTIAL_RATE1: PASS (mean=0.9999, sd=0.9910, KS p=0.7434)`. Sample files `/app/normal_samples.txt` and `/app/exponential_samples.txt` (5000 lines each) written; exponential samples all `> 0`.
